title: My English World: Grade 2 Capstone Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The word that people call you when they talk to you.
rounds.explanation: name: The word that people call you when they talk to you.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person whose job is to clean the windows of houses and shops.
rounds.explanation: window cleaner: A person whose job is to clean the windows of houses and shops.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The part at the end of your arm, with a thumb and fingers.
rounds.explanation: hand: The part at the end of your arm, with a thumb and fingers.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person who lives near you, on your street or in your building.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person you work or play with in a pair.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The star that gives Earth light and heat in the day.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My name is Amal, and I write it on my book.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: There are ten fingers on my two hands.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Leo is my partner for the reading game.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence tells you about the past?
choices: We planted a tree last term.
We are planting a tree today.
We will plant a tree in Year 3.
rounds.answer: We planted a tree last term.
rounds.explanation: Planted ends in -ed and last term is finished, so this is the past from Unit 7.

rounds.prompt: There ______ three pictures beside the booklet.
rounds.explanation: Three pictures are more than one, so we use There are, as Unit 8 taught.

rounds.prompt: Which word means a large building with many different shops inside it?
rounds.explanation: A library holds books, an apartment is rooms to live in and a hut is a small home. Only the shopping center holds many shops.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What did Amal find at the very front of her English folder?
choices: Her welcome card from Unit 1, with her name written in careful blue letters.
Her hands felt cold, and she had to breathe slowly before she spoke.
It wants you to read every page aloud before you hand the booklet in.
rounds.answer: Her welcome card from Unit 1, with her name written in careful blue letters.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Her welcome card from Unit 1, with her name written in careful blue letters.

rounds.prompt: Amal wrote that she wants to read a long chapter book next year. What does that tell you about her reading now?
choices: Her hands felt cold, and she had to breathe slowly before she spoke.
Page four, because it shows the sun and her long shadow.
She can already read shorter books by herself, so she is ready for something longer.
rounds.answer: She can already read shorter books by herself, so she is ready for something longer.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She can already read shorter books by herself, so she is ready for something longer.

rounds.prompt: The brief says your ears will find the missing full stops. What does it want you to do?
choices: Adam said it was too short, so she added two facts about planting.
It wants you to read every page aloud before you hand the booklet in.
She can already read shorter books by herself, so she is ready for something longer.
rounds.answer: It wants you to read every page aloud before you hand the booklet in.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: It wants you to read every page aloud before you hand the booklet in.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Introduce My English World
Speaking 3 — Read Aloud with Expression
rounds.target: There are ten fingers on my two hands.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — Answer a Visitor's Question
rounds.target: Which unit did this page come from?

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Nora is watering the flowers every morning before school.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I wash my hands before I eat my lunch.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Some people live in a hut made of mud and straw.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'light' mean in this unit?
choices: The brightness that lets us see things.
The word that people call you when they talk to you.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.answer: The brightness that lets us see things.
rounds.explanation: light: The brightness that lets us see things.

rounds.prompt: What does 'thirty' mean in this unit?
choices: The word that people call you when they talk to you.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
The number 30, which is three groups of ten ones.
rounds.answer: The number 30, which is three groups of ten ones.
rounds.explanation: thirty: The number 30, which is three groups of ten ones.

rounds.prompt: What does 'head' mean in this unit?
choices: An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
The word that people call you when they talk to you.
rounds.answer: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.explanation: head: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'shopping center'?
rounds.explanation: shopping center is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'spell'?
rounds.explanation: spell is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'house'?
rounds.explanation: house is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The brightness that lets us see things.
Giving water to plants so that they do not dry out.
To enjoy something or to think that it is good.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: The number 30, which is three groups of ten ones.
A small, simple house with only one or two rooms.
A person who finds out news and writes or tells stories about it.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence compares two things correctly?
choices: The metre is more long than the centimetre.
The metre is longest than the centimetre.
The metre is longer than the centimetre.
The metre long than the centimetre.
rounds.answer: The metre is longer than the centimetre.
rounds.explanation: Short describing words take -er plus than, which Unit 5 taught for measuring.

rounds.prompt: Which word tells you a position?
rounds.explanation: Under is a position word from Unit 6. Quickly tells how, butterfly names an insect and shouted is an action.

rounds.prompt: Which word is a describing word?
rounds.explanation: Bright describes the sky, as in Unit 4. Jump is an action, library names a place and quickly tells how.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: This is Amal. ______ is presenting her booklet to the class.
rounds.explanation: Amal is one girl, so we use she. Unit 1 taught this pronoun.

rounds.prompt: The firefighters ______ helping on Warta Street.
rounds.explanation: Firefighters is more than one person, so the verb is are. This is the Unit 2 pattern.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence is a command?
choices: She is pointing at page four.
Amal will point at page four.
rounds.explanation: A command starts with the plain verb and tells somebody what to do, as in Unit 3.
